title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to modify the railroad track maintenance credit.
plain_summary: This bill makes changes to a tax credit that helps railroad companies pay for fixing and maintaining their train tracks. The tax credit amount goes up from $3,500 to $6,100 for each mile of track a company owns or uses. Starting in 2026, this amount will automatically go up each year to keep up with rising prices. The bill also lets more railroad companies qualify for this tax credit by including tracks they owned as of 2024 instead of just tracks from 2015. These changes start for expenses made in 2025.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.

sections.title: Section 1  Modification of Railroad Track Maintenance Credit
sections.text: This section changes the tax credit that railroad companies can claim for maintaining their tracks. First, it increases the credit amount from three thousand five hundred dollars to six thousand one hundred dollars per mile of track maintained. Second, it adds a rule that starting in twenty twenty six, this six thousand one hundred dollar amount will go up each year based on inflation. The inflation increase will be calculated using the same method the government uses for other tax adjustments, starting from twenty twenty four as the base year. Any inflation increase that is not a round one hundred dollar amount will be rounded to the nearest one hundred dollars. Third, it updates the definition of qualified railroad track maintenance spending by changing the starting date from January first, two thousand fifteen to January first, two thousand twenty four. All of these changes apply to money spent on track maintenance in tax years that begin after December thirty first, two thousand twenty four.
